title: The Rise of Electric Vehicles
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The (0) electrification of transport is being seen as a key step in reducing carbon emissions worldwide. Sales of battery-powered cars have grown at a (17) remarkable rate in recent years, driven by consumer (18) awareness of climate change and rising fuel costs. Governments are offering (19) financial incentives to encourage drivers to switch to cleaner vehicles.||However, there are still (20) technological challenges to overcome, particularly regarding battery range and (21) charging infrastructure. Critics also point to the (22) environmental impact of mining the materials used in batteries. Despite these concerns, the overall (23) transition to electric vehicles appears (24) inevitable, as the world looks for cleaner alternatives to petrol and diesel.

example.text: The (0) ELECTRIFICATION of transport is being seen as a key step in reducing carbon emissions worldwide. (ELECTRIC)

questions.explanation: The adjective 'remarkable' is formed from 'remark' by adding the suffix '-able'.
The noun 'awareness' is formed from 'aware' by adding the suffix '-ness'.
The adjective 'financial' is formed from 'finance' by adding the suffix '-ial'.
The adjective 'technological' is formed from 'technology' by replacing '-y' with '-ical'.
The gerund/noun 'charging' is formed from 'charge' by adding the suffix '-ing'.
The adjective 'environmental' is formed from 'environment' by adding the suffix '-al'.
The noun 'transition' is formed from 'transit' by adding the suffix '-ion'.
The adjective 'inevitable' is formed from 'evitable' by adding the prefix 'in-', meaning 'cannot be avoided'.
